steps.title: Set the height to zero
steps.content:
An $x$-intercept occurs where $y=0$. For $y=f(x)+5$, solve

$$f(x)+5=0$$

Using $f(x)=4d(24x+25)+15$, this becomes

$$4d(24x+25)+20=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the intercept
steps.content: Expand the expression first, then move the constant terms to the other side. Since $d$ is nonzero, division by $96d$ is allowed, so the result gives the actual $x$-coordinate of the intercept. $$96dx+100d+20=0$$ $$96dx=-100d-20$$ $$x=\frac{-100d-20}{96d}$$ So the intercept is $(\frac{-100d-20}{96d},0)$. The answer is B.
